parsed_review.summary: CP00-302 (RP09 CRM Core, risk_class B, 40 units, RP09.P02.M07.S21-RP09.P02.M09.S18) passes independent read-only review. Coverage matches the plan binding, manifest, and registry exactly: deliverable_distribution (test:4, contract:2, implementation:18, security_audit:4, ui:6, claude_review:2, failure_recovery:4) and micro_phase_row_counts (M07:2, M08:20, M09:18) reconcile across closeout-pack-plan.json, baseline, manifest, and CRM_CORE_CP302_REQUIREMENTS. Internal consistency holds: validate-rp09-crm-core-contract.mjs deep-equals the contract against generated registry/service/validator descriptor values, and both current_pack and the historical_pack_bindings tail pin CP00-302 with upstream CP00-301 / next CP00-303 / next_subphase RP09.P02.M09.S19. Boundaries are intact: service rows are Object.freeze'd descriptor-only objects, ~40 no-write/no-runtime guards (writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, acquires_runtime_lock, etc.) are all false, validators enforce descriptor_only/runtime_execution:false/customer_safe with raw_payload excluded per row, and no fs/network/exec usage exists in CRM src. Authority boundary preserved: claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, Claude review packet read_only:true with required_model claude-opus-4-8; no promotion of Claude to final approver and no enterprise-trust claim derived from local validation. Production-ready posture is correctly conservative: manifest production_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review, valid_review_receipts empty, command-evidence exit codes all 0. CP178+ review hardening preconditions satisfied: auth preflight passed with valid JSON stdout (1228 bytes), tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob (forbidden_tools_present:false), prompt within size limit, summary_only_review_allowed:false and honored via read_tools_used inspection. Post-review artifacts (raw-output.json, review-receipt.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent because they are produced after this read-only pass, so they are not flagged as missing evidence. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic control-plane evidence/receipt handling and are not embedded in product runtime. No P0-P3 defects found.
